parentQuestion: The atomic number is equal to the number of ___.

options: The smallest unit of an element that retains its properties
scaffolds.explain: An ATOM is the SMALLEST UNIT of an element that retains its chemical properties. Atoms are the building blocks of matter.

wrongExplains.0: Molecules are made of atoms; atoms are more fundamental.
wrongExplains.2: Cells are made of molecules; atoms are smaller.
wrongExplains.3: Tissues are made of cells; atoms are much smaller.

scaffolds.q: What are the three main subatomic particles?
scaffolds.explain: The three main subatomic particles are PROTONS (positive), NEUTRONS (neutral), and ELECTRONS (negative).

wrongExplains.1: Photons are light particles; neutrons are in the nucleus.
wrongExplains.2: Ions are charged atoms; protons are subatomic particles.
wrongExplains.3: Atoms and molecules are larger structures, not subatomic.

scaffolds.q: Where are protons located in an atom?
options: In the nucleus at the center
scaffolds.explain: Protons are located IN THE NUCLEUS at the center of the atom, packed tightly with neutrons.

wrongExplains.0: Electrons orbit the nucleus; protons are in it.
wrongExplains.2: Protons are in the nucleus itself, not between structures.
wrongExplains.3: Protons are inside the atom, in the nucleus.

scaffolds.q: What charge does a proton have?
scaffolds.explain: Protons have a POSITIVE charge. This positive charge in the nucleus attracts negatively charged electrons.

wrongExplains.0: Electrons are negative; protons are positive.
wrongExplains.2: Neutrons are neutral; protons are positive.

scaffolds.q: What charge does an electron have?
scaffolds.explain: Electrons have a NEGATIVE charge. They orbit the nucleus and are attracted to the positive protons.

wrongExplains.0: Protons are positive; electrons are negative.
wrongExplains.2: Neutrons are neutral; electrons are negative.

scaffolds.q: What charge does a neutron have?
scaffolds.explain: Neutrons have NO CHARGE — they are neutral. They add mass to the nucleus without changing the charge.

wrongExplains.0: Protons are positive; neutrons are neutral.
wrongExplains.1: Electrons are negative; neutrons are neutral.

scaffolds.q: What determines which element an atom is?
scaffolds.explain: The NUMBER OF PROTONS (atomic number) determines which element an atom is. Carbon has 6 protons, oxygen has 8, etc.

wrongExplains.0: Neutron number can vary (isotopes); protons define the element.
wrongExplains.2: Electrons can be gained/lost (ions); protons define the element.
wrongExplains.3: Size varies; proton number defines the element.

options: The number of protons in an atom's nucleus
scaffolds.explain: The ATOMIC NUMBER is the NUMBER OF PROTONS in an atom's nucleus. It defines the element and appears on the periodic table.

wrongExplains.0: Neutron number can vary; atomic number is protons.
wrongExplains.2: Mass number is total particles; atomic number is just protons.
wrongExplains.3: Atomic mass includes protons and neutrons; atomic number is just protons.

scaffolds.q: The atomic number is equal to the number of ___?
scaffolds.explain: The atomic number equals the number of PROTONS. This number defines the element and never changes for that element.

wrongExplains.0: Neutrons vary in isotopes; atomic number = protons.
wrongExplains.2: Electrons can vary in ions; atomic number = protons.
wrongExplains.3: Total particles is mass number; atomic number = protons.
